title: Tunable high-Chern-number Chern insulators in rhombohedral tetralayer graphene/hBN moiré superlattices
authors: Chuanqi Zheng, Chushan Li, Ke Huang, Chenyu Zhang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Hao Yang, Dandan Guan, Liang Liu, Shiyong Wang, Yaoyi Li, Hao Zheng, Canhua Liu, Jinfeng Jia, Xueyang Song, Zhiwen Shi, Guorui Chen, Xiao Li, Tingxin Li, Xiaoxue Liu
abstract: Moiré superlattices based on rhombohedral multilayer graphene have emerged as a highly tunable platform for engineering correlated topological phases. Here, we systematically investigate the transport properties of the hole-doped side in rhombohedral tetralayer graphene/ hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) moiré superlattices across a range of twist angles and alignment orientations. Notably, we observed multiple high-Chern-number Chern insulators, including the previously reported integer Chern insulator with Chern number C = -4 at moiré filling factor v = -1 and newly discovered symmetry-broken Chern insulating states with C = +3, $\pm$2, $\pm$1 at fractional moiré fillings of v = -2.5 or -2.6. These Chern insulating states emerge in both hBN alignment, but exhibit a sensitive moiré wavelength dependence. Our findings demonstrate the exceptional tunability of these high-Chern-number states via moiré wavelength, displacement electric field and external magnetic field, underscoring the distinct topological landscape realized in hole-doped RTG/hBN moiré superlattices.
summary: This paper explores the discovery of various high-Chern-number Chern insulators in rhombohedral tetralayer graphene/hBN moiré superlattices. By investigating the hole-doped side, the authors identified new symmetry-broken states with Chern numbers such as +3, ±2, and ±1 at fractional fillings. They demonstrate that these topological phases can be precisely controlled using the moiré wavelength, displacement electric fields, and magnetic fields. This work highlights the high degree of tunability available in these specific moiré systems for engineering topological landscapes.
main_problem: The study aims to investigate the emergence and tunability of high-Chern-number Chern insulating states in hole-doped rhombohedral tetralayer graphene/hBN moiré superlattices.
main_result: The researchers discovered multiple high-Chern-number Chern insulators, including new symmetry-broken states with C = +3, ±2, and ±1 at fractional fillings, and demonstrated that these states are highly tunable via moiré wavelength, electric field, and magnetic field.
method: The study uses transport property measurements across a range of twist angles and alignment orientations in rhombohedral tetralayer graphene/hBN heterostructures.
model_or_system: The physical system is a rhombohedral tetralayer graphene (RTG) moiré superlattice formed by alignment with hexagonal boron nitride (hBN), specifically focusing on the hole-doped regime.
key_observables: Chern numbers (C), moiré filling factors (v), and transport properties.
important_parameters: Moiré wavelength, displacement electric field, external magnetic field, twist angle, and alignment orientation.
paper_structure: The paper presents an investigation of transport properties in RTG/hBN moiré superlattices, detailing the observation of various Chern insulating states at specific filling factors and discussing their dependence on external tuning parameters.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
